raw.objective: “to evaluate the influence of demographic, biochemical parameters and treatment characteristics of patients on the PK variability of TPM”
raw.covariates: Tested: gender, TOB, co-therapy with LTG, LEV, VPA, CBZ, BDZ and RIS; WT, age, renal function (MDRD), liver enzymes (AST, ALP, CHO), BIL, ALB, PRO, dose of CBZ, VPA, LTG and LEV. Included: DCBZ and MDRD on CL/F and WT on V/F
raw.observations: A one-compartment model with first order absorption and elimination was used to fit the concentrationtime TPM data. Volume of distribution of TPM was estimated at 0.575 l/kg. Daily carbamazepine dose (DCBZ) and renal function estimated by Modification of diet in renal disease (MDRD) formula significantly influenced CL/F. Mean TPM CL/F during CBZ co-therapy was 2.46 l/h (increase of 60.8%).
raw.structure: 1-compartment model with first order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: CL/F = theta1*(1+theta2*DCBZ/1000)*exp(theta3*(MDRD-95.72))*exp(eta1) V/F = theta4*WT Ka = theta5 Cobs = Cipred*(1+eps1) Notes: based on literature data, Ka was fixed to 2 h-1 due to limited data collected during absorption phase
raw.parameter_values: Theta1 = 1.53 Theta2 = 0.476 Theta3 = 0.00476 Theta4 = 0.575 Theta5 = 2 Omega1 = 0.027 Sigma1 = 0.134

extraction_target.title: Population pharmacokinetics of topiramate in adult patients with epilepsy using nonlinear mixed effects modelling.

structural_model.description: 1-compartment model with first order absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.covariates: DCBZ and MDRD on CL/F and WT on V/F
extraction_target.residual_error: CL/F = theta1*(1+theta2*DCBZ/1000)*exp(theta3*(MDRD-95.72))*exp(eta1) V/F = theta4*WT Ka = theta5 Cobs = Cipred*(1+eps1) Notes: based on literature data, Ka was fixed to 2 h-1 due to limited data collected during absorption phase
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to evaluate the influence of demographic, biochemical parameters and treatment characteristics of patients on the PK variability of TPM”

covariates.included: DCBZ and MDRD on CL/F and WT on V/F
covariates.raw: Tested: gender, TOB, co-therapy with LTG, LEV, VPA, CBZ, BDZ and RIS; WT, age, renal function (MDRD), liver enzymes (AST, ALP, CHO), BIL, ALB, PRO, dose of CBZ, VPA, LTG and LEV. Included: DCBZ and MDRD on CL/F and WT on V/F

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
